Table of Contents¶

Important Checks

  1. Import Libraries
  2. Step 1 - Data Collection and Preparation
  3. Step 2 - Feature Reduction, Engineering and Selection
  4. Step 3 - Model Selection and Optimization
  5. Step 4 - Create Pre-norm Performance Reports
  6. Step 5 - PMML File Generation
  7. Step 6 - Normalization and Norm Reports
  8. Step 7 - Supplimental Stats For Model Reports
  9. Step 8 - Data Set Preparation for QA

Some Important Checks before Model development ¶

Custom features (features starting ‘cs’, ‘csl’, ‘cb’, ‘cd’, ‘cn’) :: Check with the client before using these features for the model development.Typically, some of these features are being populated by third party vendors and needs to be discussed with the client before using them in model development

Features not to be used for model development ::

  • actimizeOperationsChallengeRecommendedInd
  • actimizeOperationsCurrentSessionChallengeStatusCode
  • actimizeOperationsChallengeResultCode
  • actimizeOperationsDelayRecomendedInd
  • actimizeOperationsPassedChallengeInSessionInd
  • actimizeChallengeResponseCdRecommenedation
  • actimizeOperationsDeclineRecommnededInd
  • actimizeDetectionResponseCdRecommenedation
  • actimizeEnrichmentIsRejectedTransaction
  • resendDueToFailureInd
  • actimizeRejectNotificationInd

  • actimizeRejectTypeCd

  • Mainentityage (Specifically its US related customers needs to be excluded ) for EMEA needs confirmation from Customer if this feature suggests a good lift

  • Alert Related features – These features may have a dependency on other third-party solutions, so need to be extra careful before using these features in the model.

Filtered Transactions

  • Check percentage of filtered and non filtered fraudulent transactions and also check if there are any KIs specific to only phase B and decide whether to includ filtered transactions or not for model development. Filtered transactions can only be used for model development, if it implemented on AMP architecture

Versioned transactions

  • Generally only first version transactions are used for the model developement(Discuss with BA once in this regard)
  • Check if any frauds are tagged for which actimizeFirstReceivedVersionInd = 0
    • If no, that is a perfect situation. Directly remove all the transactions for which actimizeFirstReceivedVersionInd=0.
    • If yes, before removing the transactions with actimizeFirstReceivedVersionInd=0, please check whether the first version of transaction is tagged as fraud using actimizeTransactionIdentity. If not, mark the first version of transaction as fraud.In short, we need to keep only 1 fraud in our sample for each unique value of actimizeTransactionIdentity.

Nulls in isfiltered field

  • Check if there are any transactions with isfiltered as NULL. If yes, get this clarified with client or PS, typically such transactions are excluded from model

Previous model features and SMO features

  • Model features from previous tuning , prefixed with "AMP_ML" and feature name starting with "aisvar_smo" shouldn't be used in the model development

PII fields

  • If any of the PII fields are found in the data, then immediately discuss such identifactions with the TAMs and such fields shouldn't be used for model development

Model Performance Report

  • Try not doing sampling on the test set and select 100% of the data for creating model performance reports

Truncation of model output

  • Truncate the probabilities up to 12 decimal places and the performance reports should be created on these truncated probabilities
  • It has been observed that when we read back the norm table csv file (which was saved after truncation logic), pd.read_csv is again modifying its values(though by a very miniscule amount) while reading it. And this is affecting the norm scores for some boundary cases. To avoid this, please run truncation logic again after reading the norm table csv and then run prob_to_norm_score function.

Data Types

  • During the feature engineering phase, make sure the data types are correctly handled, use Master feed or java files for refernce.
  • We generally consider all the KI features as numeric while developing model as we see all numbers stored in them.But some of the KIs are actually having Boolean data type in IFM instead of int/double.
  • Kindly refer sheet name IFM Variables Data Types.xlsx from the below SharePoint location
  • If some KIs are not present in the sheet, check the no. of unique values for them in the data.If a KI feature only has 1’s, 0’s and NA’s it might be a Boolean. Please contact Kushal/Vidushi to confirm their data types.

https://niceonline.sharepoint.com/sites/AnalyticsServicesHome/GTC%20IFM%20Document%20Library/Forms/AllItems.aspx?RootFolder=%2Fsites%2FAnalyticsServicesHome%2FGTC%20IFM%20Document%20Library%2FModel%20Development%2FStandard%20Scripts%20%2D%202021&View=%7B60AE1228%2DD10B%2D46F5%2D9CA4%2D994A517828E2%7D

Parquet format

  • Use parquet format instead of CSV to save the files on S3. This will help in resolving the leading zero issue. Also it is more faster than the csv.

Custom event features

  • Use ais variables instead of custom events features

Date features

  • Check minimum date for all the date features. And check if '01/01/1900' value is present in the data.
  • For this particular value of '01/01/1900', IFM system is modifying this value and adding or subtracting some minutes to this date based on the time zone of the server. So, be extra careful in handling these cases and also discuss with the QA team on the logic used to handle this.
  • Need to be extra careful while creating date diff features. Unit to be used while calculating date diff features should be ‘days’/’weeks’/’years’ rather than ‘minutes’/‘seconds’.

PMML Pipeline

  • Always do a 100% match between predictions from 1) xgboost model object and 2) PMMLPipeline object.
  • Always use PMMLPipeline object to generate predictions on Train and Test Sets for creating final performance reports, and while creating QA Model Artifacts.

Model Artifacts

Please save below model artifacts ::

  • Model Development Report in SharePoint
  • Model Documentation Report in SharePoint
  • Train/Test Sets in S3
  • Model Artifacts in zip file:
  • Norm table in csv
  • List of Raw Features csv file
  • List of Model Features csv file in correct sequence
  • XgBoost model object in json file
  • HTML file containing the logic for all the feature transformations to create Model Features from Raw Features. (For model csv approach).Ipynb file can be placed for future reference and address contingency scenarios within team .

Manual Changes in PMML

In case you are making use of 'missing' argument while fitting the XGBClassifier, a small manual modification is required in the generated PMML file mentioned below:¶

<Value value="-9999.01" property="missing"/> needs to be added inside the DataDictionary tag for each feature. Example below for 1 feature.

Original PMML File:¶

<DataField name="ki_UnusualActivityFromNewForeignCountryInVst_MobileAppRetail_Login_V2_MISS_bucketed" optype="continuous" dataType="float"/>

Modified PMML File:¶

<DataField name=" ki_UnusualActivityFromNewForeignCountryInVst_MobileAppRetail_Login_V2_MISS_bucketed " optype="continuous" dataType="float"> <Value value="-9999.01" property="missing"/> </DataField.

QA Artifacts Checks

  • Do not open normtable and before , after transformation files in an excel. If you need to open, use notepad++.
  • Opening these artifacts results in to changing precision in floating point numbers.

Please use below Model_Performance_Improvement template for Model Improvement https://niceonline.sharepoint.com/:u:/r/sites/AnalyticsServicesHome/GTC%20IFM%20Document%20Library/Model%20Development/Standard%20Scripts%20-%202021/Xsight%20Model%20development%20Template/Model_Performance_Improvement.html?csf=1&web=1

Step 1 - Data Collection and Preparation ¶

1.1 - Prepare IFM Data ¶

Input following details¶
- base activity¶
- new table name¶
- Date range¶
- columns to retain¶
In [3]:
_eda_client = EdaClient()
base_activity = "O_DCK_D"
table_name = "aditi_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jul_2022"
start_date = "2021-01-01"
end_date = "2022-07-31"
include_keys = ['actimizetransactionkey','partytype','actimizepartytypecd','accountkey','trx_day', 'actimizepartykey', 'actimizepayeekey','actimizeisfiltered', 'transferisaddeditpayee', 'actimizefirstreceivedversionind', 'actimizetransactionriskscore', 'finding', 'actimizedetectionprocessid', 'transactionnormalizeddatetime']
is_dry_run = False
In [11]:
results = _eda_client.prepare_ifm_data(base_activity=base_activity, create_table_as_name=table_name, 
                                       start_date=start_date, end_date=end_date, include_keys = include_keys,
                                       dry_run=is_dry_run)

1.2 - Exploratory Data Analysis ¶

Check total number of frauds ¶

from aditi_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jul_2022 """)

Check txns distribution in retail and commercial: For deposit activity need to check patrytype for other activity do check actimizedetectionprocessid ¶
Please run below command other than deposit activity : For Example¶

q.query(f"""select actimizedetectionprocessid,count(actimizedetectionprocessid) as Txns_Count from aditi_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jul_2022 group by actimizedetectionprocessid """)

group by partytype
""")

1.3 - Data Subset (inputs may change based on customer) ¶

In [54]:
result1 = q.create_table_as_select(table_name="aditik_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_v1",
                                select_query="""select * from aditi_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jul_2022
where actimizepartytypecd = '2' and isChampion = 1 and actimizeisfiltered = 0
and transactiontype = 'Deposit Check' and channel = 'Offline'
and transactionnormalizeddatetime >= date('2021-01-01')
and transactionnormalizeddatetime < date('2022-07-31')""")

Submitting Query:- CREATE TABLE aditik_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_v1 WITH (format = 'Parquet') AS select * from aditi_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jul_2022
where actimizepartytypecd = '2' and isChampion = 1 and actimizeisfiltered = 0
and transactiontype = 'Deposit Check' and channel = 'Offline'
and transactionnormalizeddatetime >= date('2021-01-01')
and transactionnormalizeddatetime < date('2022-07-31')
Query Submitted Successfully.
Query Execution id:- bc5d0ad8-150a-4f71-91ff-590695c96b47

Check Frauds/Cleans distribution over time ¶
In [72]:
frauds_dist_monthly_query = q.query_async(f"""select month(trx_day) as Month, year(trx_day) as Year,
min(trx_day) as Min_Date, max(trx_day) as Max_Date, count(*) as Txns, 
Sum(case when finding = 'Issue' then 1 else 0 end) as Frauds,
Sum(case when finding is null then 1 else 0 end) as Cleans
from aditik_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_v1
group by month(trx_day), year(trx_day)
order by year(trx_day), month(trx_day)""")

Submitting Query:- select month(trx_day) as Month, year(trx_day) as Year,
min(trx_day) as Min_Date, max(trx_day) as Max_Date, count(*) as Txns, 
Sum(case when finding = 'Issue' then 1 else 0 end) as Frauds,
Sum(case when finding is null then 1 else 0 end) as Cleans
from aditik_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_v1
group by month(trx_day), year(trx_day)
order by year(trx_day), month(trx_day)
Query Submitted Successfully.
Query Execution id:- 538ac14d-7cda-48ba-b900-11fe8120e780
Check the query status ¶
In [73]:
#Check query status
frauds_dist_monthly_query.status()

Getting Status of query execution id:- 538ac14d-7cda-48ba-b900-11fe8120e780
Query Status:- SUCCEEDED
Query Output Location:- s3://xsight-data-lake-us-east-1-prod/939551685/5gSrv156iir3/QueryingService/dml/538ac14d-7cda-48ba-b900-11fe8120e780.csv
In [74]:
frauds_dist_monthly = frauds_dist_monthly_query.result()

Available memory in kernel: 60916.7422 MB
In [75]:
frauds_dist_monthly.Frauds = pd.to_numeric(frauds_dist_monthly.Frauds)
frauds_dist_monthly.Cleans = pd.to_numeric(frauds_dist_monthly.Cleans)
frauds_dist_monthly['Cumm_Frauds'] = frauds_dist_monthly.Frauds.cumsum()
frauds_dist_monthly['Cumm_Cleans'] = frauds_dist_monthly.Cleans.cumsum()
frauds_dist_monthly['Cumm_Frauds_Perc'] = round(frauds_dist_monthly.Cumm_Frauds/frauds_dist_monthly.Frauds.sum(),4) * 100
frauds_dist_monthly['Cumm_Cleans_Perc'] = round(frauds_dist_monthly.Cumm_Cleans/frauds_dist_monthly.Cleans.sum(),4) * 100
frauds_dist_monthly
Out[75]:

Check weekly distribution ¶
In [76]:
frauds_dist_weekly_query = q.query_async(f"""select week(trx_day) as Week, year(trx_day) as Year,
min(trx_day) as Min_Date, max(trx_day) as Max_Date, count(*) as Txns, 
Sum(case when finding= 'Issue' then 1 else 0 end) as Frauds,
Sum(case when finding is null then 1 else 0 end) as Cleans
from aditik_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_v1
group by week(trx_day), year(trx_day)
order by year(trx_day), week(trx_day)""")

Submitting Query:- select week(trx_day) as Week, year(trx_day) as Year,
min(trx_day) as Min_Date, max(trx_day) as Max_Date, count(*) as Txns, 
Sum(case when finding= 'Issue' then 1 else 0 end) as Frauds,
Sum(case when finding is null then 1 else 0 end) as Cleans
from aditik_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_v1
group by week(trx_day), year(trx_day)
order by year(trx_day), week(trx_day)
Query Submitted Successfully.
Query Execution id:- 0fcd6cef-c0b9-488e-8276-405bbb4f9828

frauds_dist_weekly_query.status()

Query Output Location:- s3://xsight-data-lake-us-east-1-prod/939551685/5gSrv156iir3/QueryingService/dml/0fcd6cef-c0b9-488e-8276-405bbb4f9828.csv
In [78]:
frauds_dist_weekly = frauds_dist_weekly_query.result()

Available memory in kernel: 60916.3516 MB
In [79]:
frauds_dist_weekly.Frauds = pd.to_numeric(frauds_dist_weekly.Frauds)
frauds_dist_weekly.Cleans = pd.to_numeric(frauds_dist_weekly.Cleans)
frauds_dist_weekly['Cumm_Frauds'] = frauds_dist_weekly.Frauds.cumsum()
frauds_dist_weekly['Cumm_Cleans'] = frauds_dist_weekly.Cleans.cumsum()
frauds_dist_weekly['Cumm_Frauds_Perc'] = round(frauds_dist_weekly.Cumm_Frauds/frauds_dist_weekly.Frauds.sum(),4) * 100
frauds_dist_weekly['Cumm_Cleans_Perc'] = round(frauds_dist_weekly.Cumm_Cleans/frauds_dist_weekly.Cleans.sum(),4) * 100
frauds_dist_weekly

1.4 - Prepare Training and Test Set ¶

Prepare Training Set ¶
In [80]:
result1 = q.create_table_as_select(table_name="aditik_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_trset",
                                select_query="""select * from o_dck_d_11ea630a6eaee580bcec0242ac110002
where actimizepartytypecd = '2' and isChampion = 1 and actimizeisfiltered = 0
and transactiontype = 'Deposit Check' and channel = 'Offline'
and transactionnormalizeddatetime >= date('2021-01-01')
and transactionnormalizeddatetime < date('2022-02-01')""")
Check query status ¶
In [81]:
result1.status()
Prepare Test Set ¶
In [82]:
result2 = q.create_table_as_select(table_name="aditik_o_dck_d_co_feb_2022_to_jul_2022_testset",
                                select_query="""select * from o_dck_d_11ea630a6eaee580bcec0242ac110002
where actimizepartytypecd = '2' and isChampion = 1 and actimizeisfiltered = 0
and transactiontype = 'Deposit Check' and channel = 'Offline'
and transactionnormalizeddatetime >= date('2022-02-01')
and transactionnormalizeddatetime < date('2022-07-31')""")

Submitting Query:- CREATE TABLE aditik_o_dck_d_co_feb_2022_to_jul_2022_testset WITH (format = 'Parquet') AS select * from o_dck_d_11ea630a6eaee580bcec0242ac110002
where actimizepartytypecd = '2' and isChampion = 1 and actimizeisfiltered = 0
and transactiontype = 'Deposit Check' and channel = 'Offline'
and transactionnormalizeddatetime >= date('2022-02-01')
and transactionnormalizeddatetime < date('2022-07-31')
Query Submitted Successfully.

1.5 - Sampling on Training Set¶

Create a table having fraud parties list ¶
In [14]:
result = q.create_table_as_select(table_name="fraud_parties_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_trset", 
                                  select_query=f"""select distinct(actimizepartykey) from
                                  aditik_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_trset where finding = 'Issue'""")
Check query status ¶
In [15]:
result.status()
Check the contents of fraud_parties table ¶
In [16]:
fraud_parties_df = q.query("select * from fraud_parties_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_trset")
In [17]:
fraud_parties_df.shape
Create a table having clean parties list for train data ¶
In [36]:
result = q.create_table_as_select(table_name="clean_parties_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_trset",
                                select_query="""select distinct(actimizePartyKey) from aditik_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_trset
                                where finding is null and actimizepartykey not in (select actimizepartykey from fraud_parties_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_trset)""")

Submitting Query:- CREATE TABLE clean_parties_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_trset WITH (format = 'Parquet') AS select distinct(actimizetransactionkey) from aditik_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_trset
                                where finding is null and actimizetransactionkey not in (select actimizetransactionkey from fraud_parties_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_trset)
Query Submitted Successfully.

Combine the fraud_parties and sample_clean_parties table into a new table ¶
In [56]:
result = q.create_table_as_select(table_name="sample_parties_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_trset", 
                                  select_query="""select * from sample_clean_parties_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_trset
                                  union 
                                  select * from fraud_parties_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_trset""")

Submitting Query:- CREATE TABLE sample_parties_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_trset WITH (format = 'Parquet') AS select * from sample_clean_parties_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_trset
                                  union 
                                  select * from fraud_parties_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_trset
Query Submitted Successfully.

Create Sample (Restart the kernel first to clear memory) ¶
In [18]:
# Create the Querying object
from querying.querying import Querying
solution_name = "AW_IFM"
q = Querying(solution_name)
q.set_database('AwIfm_ADLService_11ea630a6eaee580bcec0242ac110002_Database')
In [19]:
sample_query = q.query_async("""select * from aditik_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_trset 
where actimizepartykey IN (select actimizepartykey from sample_parties_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_trset)""")

Submitting Query:- select * from aditik_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_trset 
where actimizetransactionkey IN (select actimizetransactionkey from sample_parties_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_trset)
Query Submitted Successfully.
Query Execution id:- 12de2b5a-8164-4925-99dc-8901c62e671b

Sample.actimizepartykey.nunique()

1.6 - Enrichment on Training and Test Set ¶

Load the Sample - Restart the kernel first to clear memory ¶
In [1]:
from dss_infra.data_access.data_access_layer import DataAccessLayer
from xss_initializer.notebook_logger_event_initializers import init_notebook_logger
init_notebook_logger()
Out[1]:
<xss_ops.logger.xsight_event_logger_operations.EventLoggerOperations at 0x7f0b429b9d80>
Note - Below enrichment is specific to training set. Pls follow the similar steps for enrichment on Test Set ¶
In [3]:
Sample =DataAccessLayer.read_parquet(relative_file_path="sampled_data/O_DCK_D_comm/Train_o_dck_d_co_jan_2021_to_jan_2022_1perc.parquet")

Available memory in kernel: 56484.1328 MB
In [6]:
Sample.shape
Out[6]:
(495020, 232)
Convert finding column to 0/1 ¶
In [7]:
import pandas as pd
pd.set_option('display.max_rows', 1000)
Sample.reset_index(inplace=True)
Sample.finding = [1 if x == 'Issue' else 0 for x in Sample.finding]
Sample.finding.value_counts()
Out[7]:
0    494810
1       210
Name: finding, dtype: int64
Party-Account Enrichment ¶
In [23]:
HoursForEnrichment = 24

FraudTxns = Sample.loc[Sample.finding == 1,['actimizepartykey','accountkey','transactionnormalizeddatetime']].reset_index(drop=True)
FraudTxns.transactionnormalizeddatetime = pd.to_datetime(FraudTxns.transactionnormalizeddatetime,format='%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S.%f')
temp = Sample.loc[:,['actimizepartykey','accountkey','transactionnormalizeddatetime','finding','index']]
temp.transactionnormalizeddatetime = pd.to_datetime(temp.transactionnormalizeddatetime,format='%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S.%f')
FraudPartyKeys = FraudTxns.actimizepartykey.to_list()
FraudPayeeKeys = FraudTxns.accountkey.to_list()
FraudTime = FraudTxns.transactionnormalizeddatetime.to_list()
EnrichedIndexes = [None]*len(temp)
counter = 0

for i in range(len(FraudPartyKeys)):
    tmp = temp.loc[(temp.actimizepartykey == FraudPartyKeys[i]) & (temp.accountkey == FraudPayeeKeys[i]) & (temp.finding == 0),['index','transactionnormalizeddatetime']].reset_index(drop=True)
    if len(tmp) > 0:
        for j in range(len(tmp)):
            time_diff = abs((tmp.loc[j,'transactionnormalizeddatetime'] - FraudTime[i]).total_seconds()/3600)
            if time_diff <= HoursForEnrichment:
                EnrichedIndexes[counter] = tmp.loc[j,'index']
                counter = counter + 1
                
EnrichedIndexes = list(set([x for x in EnrichedIndexes if x is not None]))
EnrichmentCount = len(EnrichedIndexes)
if EnrichmentCount == 0:
    print("No enrichment found")
else:
    print(str(EnrichmentCount) + " enrichments found.")
    Sample.loc[EnrichedIndexes,'finding'] = 2

Sample.drop(['index'],axis=1,inplace=True)    

9293 enrichments found.
Check the no. of enrichments and frauds ¶
In [24]:
Sample.finding.value_counts()
Out[24]:
0    485517
2      9293
1       210
Name: finding, dtype: int64
In [25]:
# Replace 2 with 1
Sample.finding.replace(2,1,inplace=True)
Sample.finding.value_counts()
Out[25]:
0    494810
1       210
Name: finding, dtype: int64

1.7 - Initial Feature Reduction ¶

In [26]:
# import pipeline
from model_template_pipeline_classes import *
from PMML_Interpipes_template_Unified_pmml_aux import *
In [27]:
def cleansing_steps(pipeline):
    pipeline.add_step(RemoveNulls()) 
    pipeline.add_step(RemoveZeroVariance())
    return pipeline
In [28]:
def create_pipeline(pipeline):
    # Date Cleansing
    pipeline.add_step(RemoveVset())
    #pipeline.add_step(RemoveScore())
    #pipeline.add_step(RemoveDuplicateCols())
    #pipeline.add_step(ConvertToNum())
    cleansing_steps(pipeline)
    return pipeline
In [29]:
Sample_org = Sample.copy()
y_Sample= Sample['finding']
In [30]:
# initiate pipeline & add requiered steps
clean_data = XSightPipeline()
pipeline = create_pipeline(clean_data)
In [31]:
final_data = pipeline.fit_transform(Sample_org.copy(), y_Sample)

1.9 - Change ADL headers as per IFM format ¶

In [38]:
data=CrossAccountAccess.change_dataframe_headers_adl(data,'O_DCK_D')
In [5]:
DateFeatures_BasedOnName = list(data.columns[data.columns.str.contains('date',case=False)]) + list(data.columns[data.columns.str.startswith('cd')])
DateFeatures_BasedOnDataType = data.select_dtypes(include=['datetime64']).columns.tolist()
Difference = list(set(DateFeatures_BasedOnDataType) - set(DateFeatures_BasedOnName))
AllDateFeatures = DateFeatures_BasedOnName + Difference
#print('AllDateFeatures -',len(AllDateFeatures))

for i in AllDateFeatures:
    #print(i)
    data[i] = data[i].astype(str).str.replace("(\d{1,4})-(\d{1,2})-(\d{1,2}) (.*?).(\d\d\d)$", "\\3/\\2/\\1 \\4:\\5")
                
for i in AllDateFeatures:
    #print(i)
    data[i] = data[i].astype(str).str.replace("(\d{1,4})-(\d{1,2})-(\d{1,2})$", "\\3/\\2/\\1")

for i in AllDateFeatures:
    #print(i)
    data[i] = data[i].astype(str).str.replace("(\d{1,4})-(\d{1,2})-(\d{1,2}) (.*?)$", "\\3/\\2/\\1 \\4")
    
data[AllDateFeatures] = data[AllDateFeatures].replace(['NaT','nan','None'],np.NAN) 

/tmp/ipykernel_30096/3497572285.py:9: FutureWarning: The default value of regex will change from True to False in a future version.
  data[i] = data[i].astype(str).str.replace("(\d{1,4})-(\d{1,2})-(\d{1,2}) (.*?).(\d\d\d)$", "\\3/\\2/\\1 \\4:\\5")
/tmp/ipykernel_30096/3497572285.py:13: FutureWarning: The default value of regex will change from True to False in a future version.
  data[i] = data[i].astype(str).str.replace("(\d{1,4})-(\d{1,2})-(\d{1,2})$", "\\3/\\2/\\1")
/tmp/ipykernel_30096/3497572285.py:17: FutureWarning: The default value of regex will change from True to False in a future version.
  data[i] = data[i].astype(str).str.replace("(\d{1,4})-(\d{1,2})-(\d{1,2}) (.*?)$", "\\3/\\2/\\1 \\4")

1.10 - Correct data types and dates formatting ¶

In [8]:
ifm_data_types = pd.read_csv('IFMFieldsDataTypes.csv')
data_types = xsight_xss.get_data_type_table(data)
data_types = data_types.merge(ifm_data_types.dropna(),left_on = 'feature_name', right_on = 'Field' ,how='left') 
data_types = data_types.rename(columns={"Data Type": "ifm_data_type"}) 
ki_cols = data.columns[data.columns.str.startswith('ki_')].tolist()
data_types.ifm_data_type = np.where(data_types.feature_name.isin(ki_cols), 'Numeric', data_types.ifm_data_type)
data_types.ifm_data_type.value_counts(dropna=False)
Out[8]:
Numeric    132
String      60
NaN         21
Boolean     19
Name: ifm_data_type, dtype: int64
In [9]:
# Check the NaN features once and manually assign the data types to one of 'String', 'Numeric' or 'Boolean'
data_types.loc[data_types.ifm_data_type.isna(),'feature_name']
categorical_features = data_types[(data_types.ifm_data_type == 'String') | (data_types.ifm_data_type.isna())]['feature_name'].tolist()
len(categorical_features)

Manually type cast the features (if needed) ¶

In [ ]:
#DataAccessLayer.save_as_parquet(data_frame=data,
#                            relative_file_path="sampled_data/O_DCK_D_commercial/XYZ.parquet")

Step 2 - Feature Reduction, Engineering and Selection ¶

2.1 - Lift analysis ¶

Lift analysis Categorical ¶
In [12]:
DateFeatures1 = list(data.columns[data.columns.str.contains('date',case=False)]) + list(data.columns[data.columns.str.startswith('cd')])
DateFeatures2 = data.select_dtypes(include=['datetime64']).columns.tolist()
MissedDateFeatures = list(set(DateFeatures2) - set(DateFeatures1))
DateFeatures = DateFeatures1 + MissedDateFeatures
NumericFeatures = data.drop(DateFeatures,axis=1).select_dtypes(include=['int64','float64']).columns.tolist()
CharacterFeatures = data.drop(DateFeatures,axis=1).select_dtypes(include=['object']).columns.tolist()
# Include date diff features as well in the NumericFeatures list
NumericFeaturesCardinality = data[NumericFeatures].nunique(dropna=False)
CharacterFeaturesCardinality = data[CharacterFeatures].nunique(dropna=False)
FeaturesForContinuousLift = NumericFeaturesCardinality[NumericFeaturesCardinality >= 100].index.to_list()
FeaturesForCategoricalLift = CharacterFeaturesCardinality[CharacterFeaturesCardinality < 100].index.to_list() + NumericFeaturesCardinality[NumericFeaturesCardinality < 100].index.to_list()
In [15]:
# Create pandas Dataframe with features required for categorical lift, with additional 2 columns - actimizepartykey and fraud
df = data[FeaturesForCategoricalLift+['actimizePartyKey']]
lift_report_categorical = lift_categorical(df,'finding','actimizePartyKey')

Features selected from lift analysis ¶

orig_vars = ['fraud',
'accountNumberOfRelatedParties',
'phaseBModelTrxScore',
'partyState',
'actimizeIsRequestedAmountAsEnteredRounded',
'payeeRoutingType',
'requestedAmountNormalizedCurrency',
'actimizeTotalRollingDepositAmount',
'actimizeOperationsTimeSincePartyConfirmedFraudAlerts',
'accountLedgerBalance',
'accountAvailableBalance',
'trxAccountCurrentBalance',
'actimizeOperationsTimeSincePartyOpenAlerts',
'trxAccountUncollectedBalance',
'depositTotalCreditItemNormalizedAmount',
'accountAddressUpdateDate',
'accountInfoUpdateDate',
'accountOpenDate',
'partyAddressUpdateDate',
'partyEmailUpdateDate',
'transactionNormalizedDateTime'
]
In [12]:
df_train = Train_data[orig_vars]
In [13]:
df_test = Test_data[orig_vars]

2.2 - Feature selection using Boruta ¶

In [ ]:
# Note - Date features needs to be excluded before runing this code.
# load X and y
Data = df_train
X = Data.drop(['fraud'],axis=1)
y = Data.fraud
X.shape
# Missing Values Imputation
print('Missing Values Imputation')
print('Total Missing Values in Data -->',X.isna().sum().sum())
# Check list of categorical and continuous features
print('Check no. of categorical and continuous features')
numerical_cols = [cname for cname in X.columns if X[cname].dtype in ['int64', 'float64']]
cat_cols = [cname for cname in X.columns if X[cname].dtype == "object"]
print('Total Numeric Fetaures -->',len(numerical_cols))
print('Total Categorical Fetaures -->',len(cat_cols))
# Impute missing values of numeric features to -9999
X.loc[:,numerical_cols] = X.loc[:,numerical_cols].fillna(-9999.01)
# Impute missing values of categorical features to 'MISS'
X.loc[:,cat_cols] = X.loc[:,cat_cols].fillna("MISS")
# Encode Categorical features
# Check cardinality
print('Check cardinality of Categorical features')
cat_cols_nunique = list(map(lambda col:X[col].nunique(),cat_cols))
cat_cols_nunique
d = dict(zip(cat_cols, cat_cols_nunique))
# Print number of unique entries by column, in ascending order
sorted(d.items(),key=lambda x:x[1])
low_cardinality_cols = [col for col in cat_cols if X[col].nunique() < 20]
high_cardinality_cols = list(set(cat_cols)-set(low_cardinality_cols))
print('Encode Categorical features')
# One Hot Encode Low Cardinality Features

OH_encoder = OneHotEncoder(sparse=False)
OH_cols_X = pd.DataFrame(OH_encoder.fit_transform(X[low_cardinality_cols]))
OH_cols_X.index = X.index
OH_encoder.get_feature_names(low_cardinality_cols)
OH_cols_X.columns = OH_encoder.get_feature_names(low_cardinality_cols)
# Label Encode High Cardinality Features

label_encoder = LabelEncoder()
label_X = X.loc[:,high_cardinality_cols].copy()
for col in high_cardinality_cols:
    label_X[col] = label_encoder.fit_transform(X[col])
num_X = X.loc[:,numerical_cols]
num_X.shape
label_X.shape
OH_cols_X.shape
# Concatenate
FinalData = pd.concat([num_X,label_X,OH_cols_X],axis=1)
FinalData.shape
print('Running Boruta')
# load X and y
# BorutaPy accepts numpy arrays only, hence the .values attribute
X = FinalData.values
y = Data.fraud.values
# define random forest classifier, with utilising all cores and
# sampling in proportion to y labels
rf = RandomForestClassifier(n_jobs=-1, class_weight='balanced', max_depth=5)
# define Boruta feature selection method
feat_selector = BorutaPy(rf, n_estimators='auto', verbose=2, random_state=1, max_iter = 150, perc = 90)
feat_selector.fit(X, y)
ConfirmedFeatures = FinalData.columns[feat_selector.support_].to_list()
TentativeFeatures = FinalData.columns[feat_selector.support_weak_].to_list()
print('Confirmed Features -->',len(ConfirmedFeatures))
print('Tentative Features -->',len(TentativeFeatures))
print(ConfirmedFeatures)

495016 2018-04-15 2020-01-17 2001-11-14 2018-04-15 2009-01-09 15:29:20.443 2021-02-01 11:02:44

495020 rows × 6 columns

Before feature engineering, need to change the format of date variables ¶
Note : It is observed that all the dates in IFM are coming in "%d/%m/%Y %H:%M:%S:%f" format, even though they may look different while querying the data in X-Sight Studio.¶
So, kindly make a note to change the format for every date to "%d/%m/%Y %H:%M:%S:%f" before starting with feature engineering, and also in Before Transform file shared to QA. Sample code below:¶
For dates with dd/mm/yyyy hh:mm:ss but missing microseconds:¶

Train['accountAddressUpdateDate'] = Train['accountAddressUpdateDate'] + ' 00:00:00:000'

In [19]:
DateFeatures_BasedOnName = list(df_train.columns[df_train.columns.str.contains('date',case=False)]) + list(df_train.columns[df_train.columns.str.startswith('cd')])
DateFeatures_BasedOnDataType = df_train.select_dtypes(include=['datetime64']).columns.tolist()
Difference = list(set(DateFeatures_BasedOnDataType) - set(DateFeatures_BasedOnName))
AllDateFeatures = DateFeatures_BasedOnName + Difference
print('AllDateFeatures -',len(AllDateFeatures))

for i in AllDateFeatures:
    print(i)
    df_train[i] = df_train[i].astype(str).str.replace("(\d{1,4})-(\d{1,2})-(\d{1,2}) (.*?).(\d\d\d)$", "\\3/\\2/\\1 \\4:\\5")
                
for i in AllDateFeatures:
    print(i)
    df_train[i] = df_train[i].astype(str).str.replace("(\d{1,4})-(\d{1,2})-(\d{1,2})$", "\\3/\\2/\\1")

for i in AllDateFeatures:
    print(i)
    df_train[i] = df_train[i].astype(str).str.replace("(\d{1,4})-(\d{1,2})-(\d{1,2}) (.*?)$", "\\3/\\2/\\1 \\4")
    
    
df_train['accountAddressUpdateDate'] = df_train['accountAddressUpdateDate'] + ' 00:00:00:000'
df_train['accountInfoUpdateDate']= df_train['accountInfoUpdateDate'] + ' 00:00:00:000'
df_train['accountOpenDate'] = df_train['accountOpenDate'] + ' 00:00:00:000'
df_train['partyAddressUpdateDate'] = df_train['partyAddressUpdateDate'] + ' 00:00:00:000'
 
    
df_train[AllDateFeatures] = df_train[AllDateFeatures].replace(['NaT','nan','None'],np.NAN)

df_train[AllDateFeatures] = df_train[AllDateFeatures].replace(['NaT 00:00:00:000'],np.NAN)
df_train[AllDateFeatures] = df_train[AllDateFeatures].replace(['nan 00:00:00:000'],np.NAN)
df_train[AllDateFeatures] = df_train[AllDateFeatures].replace(['None 00:00:00:000'],np.NAN)    
    

AllDateFeatures - 6

In [21]:
ifm_data_types = pd.read_csv('IFMFieldsDataTypes.csv')
data_types = xsight_xss.get_data_type_table(df_train)
data_types = data_types.merge(ifm_data_types.dropna(),left_on = 'feature_name', right_on = 'Field' ,how='left') 
data_types = data_types.rename(columns={"Data Type": "ifm_data_type"}) 
ki_cols = df_train.columns[df_train.columns.str.startswith('ki_')].tolist()
data_types.ifm_data_type = np.where(data_types.feature_name.isin(ki_cols), 'Numeric', data_types.ifm_data_type)
data_types.ifm_data_type.value_counts(dropna=False)

DateFeatures_BasedOnDataType = df_test.select_dtypes(include=['datetime64']).columns.tolist()
Difference = list(set(DateFeatures_BasedOnDataType) - set(DateFeatures_BasedOnName))
AllDateFeatures = DateFeatures_BasedOnName + Difference

for i in AllDateFeatures:
    print(i)
    df_test[i] = df_test[i].astype(str).str.replace("(\d{1,4})-(\d{1,2})-(\d{1,2}) (.*?).(\d\d\d)$", "\\3/\\2/\\1 \\4:\\5")
                
for i in AllDateFeatures:
    print(i)

    df_test[i] = df_test[i].astype(str).str.replace("(\d{1,4})-(\d{1,2})-(\d{1,2}) (.*?)$", "\\3/\\2/\\1 \\4")

ifm_data_types = pd.read_csv('IFMFieldsDataTypes.csv')
data_types = xsight_xss.get_data_type_table(df_test)
data_types = data_types.merge(ifm_data_types.dropna(),left_on = 'feature_name', right_on = 'Field' ,how='left') 
data_types = data_types.rename(columns={"Data Type": "ifm_data_type"}) 
ki_cols = df_test.columns[df_test.columns.str.startswith('ki_')].tolist()
data_types.ifm_data_type = np.where(data_types.feature_name.isin(ki_cols), 'Numeric', data_types.ifm_data_type)
data_types.ifm_data_type.value_counts(dropna=False)
Out[26]:
Numeric    10
String      8
NaN         1
Boolean     1
Name: ifm_data_type, dtype: int64
In [27]:
# Check the NaN features once and manually assign the data types to one of 'String', 'Numeric' or 'Boolean'
data_types.loc[data_types.ifm_data_type.isna(),'feature_name']
categorical_features = data_types[(data_types.ifm_data_type == 'String') | (data_types.ifm_data_type.isna())]['feature_name'].tolist()
len(categorical_features)
Out[27]:
0    fraud
Name: feature_name, dtype: object
Out[27]:
9
In [28]:
numerical_features =  data_types[data_types.ifm_data_type.isin(['Numeric','Boolean']) ]['feature_name'].tolist()
len(numerical_features)
##Conversion of numerical_features to float
df_test[numerical_features] = df_test[numerical_features].astype(float)
Out[28]:
11
In [29]:
y_test=df_test.fraud
Concatenate train and test data ¶
In [30]:
df_train['flag']= 'Tr'
In [31]:
df_test['flag']= 'Ts'
In [32]:
Full_data = pd.concat([df_train,df_test],axis=0)

2.4 - Feature Engineering ¶

Feature Transformation template

  • If you want to use feature transformation other than below then please use Export_Engineered_Features_XsightPipeline_1stJune2022 template from sharepoint link. https://niceonline.sharepoint.com/:u:/r/sites/AnalyticsServicesHome/GTC%20IFM%20Document%20Library/Model%20Development/Standard%20Scripts%20-%202021/Xsight%20Model%20development%20Template/Export_Engineered_Features_XsightPipeline_1stJune2022.html?csf=1&web=1
Transformation on Train and Test Data ¶
In [33]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from xgboost import XGBClassifier
from sklearn2pmml import sklearn2pmml
from sklearn2pmml.pipeline import PMMLPipeline
from dss_infra.data_access.data_access_layer import DataAccessLayer
from xss_initializer.notebook_logger_event_initializers import init_notebook_logger
init_notebook_logger()
from xsight_ml_pipeline.xsight_pipeline import XSightPipeline
from xsight_export_engineering_features.xsight_transformations_lib import TransformationsLibrary
from xsight_export_engineering_features.xsight_transformations_lib import ModelCsvLogger
from custom_transformations_library import CustomTransformationsLibrary
Out[33]:
<xss_ops.logger.xsight_event_logger_operations.EventLoggerOperations at 0x7f77b10610c0>
In [34]:
xsight_pipeline = XSightPipeline()
tl = TransformationsLibrary()
logger= ModelCsvLogger()
cs = CustomTransformationsLibrary()
Missing Value Imputation ¶
In [35]:
Features =[
'accountNumberOfRelatedParties',
'phaseBModelTrxScore',
'partyState',
'actimizeIsRequestedAmountAsEnteredRounded',
'payeeRoutingType',
'requestedAmountNormalizedCurrency',
'actimizeTotalRollingDepositAmount',
'accountAvailableBalance',
'actimizeOperationsTimeSincePartyConfirmedFraudAlerts',
'accountLedgerBalance',
'actimizeOperationsTimeSincePartyOpenAlerts',
'trxAccountUncollectedBalance',
'depositTotalCreditItemNormalizedAmount'
]
In [36]:
xsight_pipeline = XSightPipeline()
xsight_pipeline.add_step(tl.missing_sub,features=Features,
                         substitute_value=-9999.01,suffix='_MISS',_suffix='_MISS', 
                         append_features=True,logger=logger)
my_model_transformations_1 = xsight_pipeline.fit_transform(Full_data)

missing_subs_value = -9999.01
missing_subs_value = -9999.01
missing_subs_value = -9999.01

In [39]:
DateFeatures=['accountAddressUpdateDate','accountInfoUpdateDate','accountOpenDate','partyAddressUpdateDate','partyEmailUpdateDate','transactionNormalizedDateTime']
Do missing value imputation according to the existing date formats in the data ¶
In [40]:
xsight_pipeline = XSightPipeline()
xsight_pipeline.add_step(tl.missing_sub,features=DateFeatures,
                         substitute_value="01/01/1900 00:00:00:000",suffix='_MISS',_suffix='_MISS', 
                         append_features=True,logger=logger)
my_model_transformations_1 = xsight_pipeline.fit_transform(my_model_transformations_1)

Date difference ¶
In [42]:
xsight_pipeline = XSightPipeline()
xsight_pipeline.add_step(cs.custom_date_diff,features=['transactionNormalizedDateTime_MISS','accountAddressUpdateDate_MISS'],
                         out_var = 'diff_accountAddressUpdateDate',
                         format1 = '%d/%m/%Y %H:%M:%S:%f',
                         substitute_value1 = '01/01/1900 00:00:00:000',
                         format2 = '%d/%m/%Y %H:%M:%S:%f',
                         substitute_value2 = '01/01/1900 00:00:00:000',
                         out_substitute_value=-9999.01,
                         timePeriod='Day',
                         logger=logger)
my_model_transformations_1 = xsight_pipeline.fit_transform(my_model_transformations_1)
In [43]:

                         substitute_value1 = '01/01/1900 00:00:00:000',

Presence of an element in a list ¶
In [52]:
xsight_pipeline = XSightPipeline()
xsight_pipeline.add_step(tl.is_element,features=['partyState_MISS'],
                         value = ['SC','TN','MA','KY','NJ','ME','GA','CO','FL','PA','IL','NY','CA'],is_value_column=False,
                         append_features=True,suffix="_ind",_suffix="_ind",logger=logger)
my_model_transformations_1 = xsight_pipeline.fit_transform(my_model_transformations_1)
In [53]:
my_model_transformations_1['partyState_MISS_ind']=np.where(my_model_transformations_1['partyState_MISS_ind']==True,1,0)
my_model_transformations_1.partyState_MISS_ind.value_counts()
Out[53]:
0    355233
1    253721
Name: partyState_MISS_ind, dtype: int64
ifelse conditions ¶
In [54]:
def ifelse_feature(df,condition,outVarName,**kwargs):
    if isinstance(df, pd.DataFrame):
        df[outVarName] = df.apply(lambda X:eval(condition),axis=1)
        return df
In [55]:
xsight_pipeline = XSightPipeline()
xsight_pipeline.add_step(ifelse_feature,features=['payeeRoutingType_MISS'],
                         condition="1 if X[0] == 'BIC' else 0",
                         outVarName='payeeRoutingType_ind',logger=logger)
my_model_transformations_1 = xsight_pipeline.fit_transform(my_model_transformations_1)
In [56]:
my_model_transformations_1.payeeRoutingType_ind.value_counts()
Out[56]:
0    426840
1    182114
Name: payeeRoutingType_ind, dtype: int64

logger.log(block_name="Enrichment",
           var_name="payeeRoutingType_ind",
           condition="input_df$payeeRoutingType_MISS == 'BIC'",
           true_value="1",
           false_value="0",
           type='',
           expose_to_adl="1")
In [58]:
train=my_model_transformations_1.loc[(my_model_transformations_1.flag=='Tr')].reset_index(drop = True)
In [59]:
test=my_model_transformations_1.loc[(my_model_transformations_1.flag=='Ts')].reset_index(drop = True)
In [60]:
cols_to_keep=['actimizeIsRequestedAmountAsEnteredRounded_MISS',
'phaseBModelTrxScore_MISS',
'accountLedgerBalance_MISS',
'partyState_MISS_ind',
'actimizeTotalRollingDepositAmount_MISS',
'requestedAmountNormalizedCurrency_MISS',
'trxAccountUncollectedBalance_MISS',
'payeeRoutingType_ind',
'diff_partyAddressUpdateDate_floor',
'accountAvailableBalance_MISS',
'actimizeOperationsTimeSincePartyConfirmedFraudAlerts_MISS',
'diff_accountOpenDate_floor',
'actimizeOperationsTimeSincePartyOpenAlerts_MISS',
'diff_accountAddressUpdateDate_floor',
'accountNumberOfRelatedParties_MISS',
'depositTotalCreditItemNormalizedAmount_MISS',
'diff_partyEmailUpdateDate_floor',
'diff_accountInfoUpdateDate_floor'
]
In [61]:
X_train = train[cols_to_keep]
X_train = X_train.astype(float)
In [62]:
X_test = test[cols_to_keep]
X_test = X_test.astype(float)
In [63]:
# check correlation after feature engineering
corr_df = correlationReport(X_train,0.9)

Step 3 - Model Selection and Optimization ¶

3.1 - Hyperparameters Optimization - Grid Search - XGBoost ¶

Create Custom Folds ¶
In [59]:
X_train['actimizePartyKey']= Train_data['actimizePartyKey']
X_train['fraud']= Train_data['fraud']
In [60]:
#Define no. of folds
folds = 5
Sample = X_train
AllParties = Sample.actimizePartyKey.unique()
FraudParties = Sample.actimizePartyKey[Sample.fraud == 1].unique()
CleanParties = np.setdiff1d(AllParties, FraudParties)
print("Total Parties in Sample -",len(AllParties))
print("Total Fraud Parties in Sample -",len(FraudParties))
print("Total Clean Parties in Sample -",len(CleanParties))
# Shuffle FraudParties and CleanParties
np.random.seed(123)
np.random.shuffle(FraudParties)
np.random.seed(123)
np.random.shuffle(CleanParties)
FraudPartyIndicesByFold = np.array_split(range(len(FraudParties)),folds)
CleanPartyIndicesByFold = np.array_split(range(len(CleanParties)),folds)
FraudPartyListByFold = [FraudParties[foldIndices] for foldIndices in FraudPartyIndicesByFold]
CleanPartyListByFold = [CleanParties[foldIndices] for foldIndices in CleanPartyIndicesByFold]
Sample_Indices_FraudPartyByFold = [Sample.index[Sample.actimizePartyKey.isin(fraudPartyListByFold)].tolist() for fraudPartyListByFold in FraudPartyListByFold]
Sample_Indices_CleanPartyByFold = [Sample.index[Sample.actimizePartyKey.isin(cleanPartyListByFold)].tolist() for cleanPartyListByFold in CleanPartyListByFold]
Sample_IndicesByFold = [sample_Indices_FraudPartyByFold+sample_Indices_CleanPartyByFold for sample_Indices_FraudPartyByFold,sample_Indices_CleanPartyByFold in zip(Sample_Indices_FraudPartyByFold,Sample_Indices_CleanPartyByFold)]
Report = pd.DataFrame({'Fold':[np.NAN]*folds,
                       'Total_Parties_Train':[np.NAN]*folds,
                       'Total_Parties_Test':[np.NAN]*folds,
                       'Total_Frauds_Train':[np.NAN]*folds,
                       'Total_Frauds_Test':[np.NAN]*folds})
custom_cv_iterable = []
for i in range(folds):
    testIndices = np.array(Sample_IndicesByFold[i])
    trainIndices = np.array(Sample.index[~Sample.index.isin(testIndices)])
    custom_cv_iterable.append( (trainIndices, testIndices) )
    Report.iloc[i,0] = i+1
    Report.iloc[i,1] = Sample.actimizePartyKey[Sample.index.isin(trainIndices)].nunique()
    Report.iloc[i,2] = Sample.actimizePartyKey[Sample.index.isin(testIndices)].nunique()
    Report.iloc[i,3] = Sample.fraud[Sample.index.isin(trainIndices)].sum()
    Report.iloc[i,4] = Sample.fraud[Sample.index.isin(testIndices)].sum()
print("Summary of Folds:\n")
Report

Total Parties in Sample - 10421
Total Fraud Parties in Sample - 82
Total Clean Parties in Sample - 10339
Summary of Folds:
Out[60]:
Fold Total_Parties_Train Total_Parties_Test Total_Frauds_Train Total_Frauds_Test
0 1.0 8336.0 2085.0 186.0 24.0
1 2.0 8336.0 2085.0 156.0 54.0
2 3.0 8337.0 2084.0 163.0 47.0
3 4.0 8337.0 2084.0 180.0 30.0
4 5.0 8338.0 2083.0 155.0 55.0
Grid Search ¶
In [61]:
#Prepare the data set for HPO
Y = y_train
X = X_train[cols_to_keep]
X.shape
Out[61]:
(495020, 18)
In [62]:
# Check no. of cores
import psutil
psutil.cpu_count()
Out[62]:
16
HPO - Adjust the parameters as per the requirement ¶
In [ ]:
xgb = XGBClassifier()
# Define the grid
params = {'learning_rate':[0.1,0.2,0.3],
          'min_child_weight': [1,5,7],
          'gamma': [0,1],
          'subsample': [0.6,0.8,1],
          'colsample_bytree': [0.6,0.8,1],
          'max_depth': [3,4,5],
          'n_estimators': [40,50,60,70,80,90,100]
         }
grid_search = GridSearchCV(estimator=xgb, param_grid=params, 
                    scoring=['roc_auc','average_precision','recall','precision','f1'], 
                    n_jobs=-1, cv=custom_cv_iterable, refit=False, verbose=4)
grid_search.fit(X, Y)
Consolidate the Grid Search results in a dataframe ¶
In [66]:
# Consolidate the Grid Search results in a dataframe
grid_search_params = pd.DataFrame(grid_search.cv_results_["params"])
grid_search_metrics = pd.DataFrame(grid_search.cv_results_)[['mean_test_roc_auc','mean_test_average_precision',
                                                             'mean_test_recall','mean_test_precision',
                                                             'mean_test_f1','std_test_roc_auc',
                                                             'std_test_average_precision','std_test_recall',
                                                             'std_test_precision','std_test_f1',
                                                             'rank_test_roc_auc','rank_test_average_precision',
                                                             'rank_test_recall','rank_test_precision',
                                                             'rank_test_f1','mean_fit_time',
                                                             'mean_score_time']]
gridSearchResults = pd.concat([grid_search_params,grid_search_metrics],axis=1)
gridSearchResults.shape
Out[66]:
(12, 24)
In [68]:
# View the results
view(gridSearchResults)
Save the grid results in to csv ¶
In [69]:
#gridSearchResults.to_csv('gridSearchResults_O_DCK_D_commercial.csv',index=False)

3.2 - Fit Xgboost Model with best parameters ¶

In [ ]:
# # Call 3 times with different values in importance_type - 'gain', 'weight' and 'cover'
xgb = XGBClassifier(objective='binary:logistic',
                    booster='gbtree',
                    learning_rate =0.2,
                    random_state=2,
                    subsample=1,
                    colsample_bytree=0.85,
                    gamma=1,
                    max_depth=3,
                    min_child_weight=7,
                    n_estimators = 45,
                   missing = -9999.01)
xgb.fit(X_train.copy(), y_train)
In [ ]:
gain = xgb.feature_importances_
weight = xgb.feature_importances_
cover = xgb.feature_importances_
In [ ]:
xgb_feature_importance = pd.DataFrame({'Feature':Model_Features_List,'Gain':gain,'Weight':weight,'Cover':cover})
xgb_feature_importance.sort_values(by=['Gain'],ascending=False,inplace=True)
xgb_feature_importance

3.3 - Save the model ¶

In [106]:
xgb.save_model('KeyBank_O_DCK_D_1_Co_ModelObject.json')

3.4 - Predictions ¶

Predict on Training data ¶
In [65]:
xgb_pred_train = xgb.predict_proba(X_train.copy())[:,1]
Predict on Testing data ¶
In [66]:
xgb_pred_test = xgb.predict_proba(X_test.copy())[:,1]

Step 4 - Create Pre-norm Performance Reports ¶

Please make sure to truncate the probabilities to 12 decimal places for creating the performance reports ¶

In [67]:
# truncate train prediction values to 12 decimal digits
xgb_pred_train = trunc(xgb_pred_train, decs=12)
In [68]:
# truncate test prediction values to 12 decimal digits
xgb_pred_test = trunc(xgb_pred_test, decs=12)
Fraud parties for Training set ¶
In [82]:
fr_party_train=Train_data.actimizePartyKey[Train_data.fraud==1]

5.1 - Fit the PMML Pipeline ¶

In [70]:

    ("classifier", XGBClassifier(objective='binary:logistic',booster='gbtree',learning_rate =0.2,random_state=2,subsample=1,colsample_bytree=0.85,gamma=1,max_depth=3,min_child_weight=7,n_estimators = 45,missing = -9999.01))
])
In [71]:

Step 6 - Normalization and Norm Reports ¶

6.1 - Normalization based on Daily Alerts ¶

In [76]:
def norm_table(df,probs,totalDays):
    """
    Creates norm table based on how many alerts a bank would like to handle at each score.
    Full Test Set needs to be used here i.e. 100% transactions, not the sampled one.
    
    Parameters:
    df : pandas Dataframe having 2 columns:
        1. Score : norm score
        2. Alerts : no. of alerts to be handled
    probs : numpy array of probabilities
    totalDays : data duration in days
    
    Returns
    -------
    pandas Dataframe having below columns:
    1. Score : norm score
    2. scoreThreshold : probability threshold
    
    Usage Example
    -------------
    df = pd.DataFrame({'Score':np.arange(100,-1,-5),'Alerts':np.array([1,5,10,20,30,40,50,70,100,120,150,180,220,250,300,500,750,1000,1500,1700,2000])})
    normTable = norm_table(df,probs,totalDays) 
    """
    df = df.copy()
    ProbAlertsDF = pd.DataFrame({'Prob':probs})
    ProbAlertsDF.sort_values(by=['Prob'],ascending=False,inplace=True)
    ProbAlertsDF['Txn_Count'] = np.arange(1,len(probs)+1)
    ProbAlertsDF['DailyAlerts'] = round(ProbAlertsDF['Txn_Count']/totalDays,2)
    df.sort_values(by='Score',inplace=True)
    Alerts = round(df.Alerts,2)
    df['scoreThreshold'] = Alerts.apply(lambda x: ProbAlertsDF.Prob.loc[abs(x-ProbAlertsDF.DailyAlerts).idxmin()])
    normTable = df.loc[:,['Score','scoreThreshold']]
    if (normTable.Score == 0).any():
        normTable.loc[normTable.Score == 0,'scoreThreshold'] = -9999999
    else:
        normTable = pd.concat(normTable,pd.DataFrame({'Score':0,'scoreThreshold':-9999999}))
    return normTable

##  In case of transaction level sampling please use below Normtable functions

def norm_table_sampled_data(df,probs,isFraud,totalDays,ratio_clean_sampling):
    """
    Creates norm table based on how many alerts a bank would like to handle at each score.
    
    Parameters:
    df : pandas Dataframe having 2 columns:
        1. Score : norm score
        2. Alerts : no. of alerts to be handled
    probs : numpy array of probabilities
    isFraud : numpy array of 1/0 fraud tags
    totalDays : data duration in days
    
    Returns
    -------
    pandas Dataframe having below columns:
    1. Score : norm score
    2. scoreThreshold : probability threshold
    
    Usage Example
    -------------
    df = pd.DataFrame({'Score':np.arange(100,-1,-5),'Alerts':np.array([1,5,10,20,30,40,50,70,100,120,150,180,220,250,300,500,750,1000,1500,1700,2000])})
    normTable = norm_table_sampled_data(df,probs,isFraud,totalDays,ratio_clean_sampling) 
    """
    df = df.copy()
    ProbAlertsDF = pd.DataFrame({'Prob':probs,'Fraud':isFraud})
    ProbAlertsDF.sort_values(by=['Prob'],ascending=False,inplace=True)
    ProbAlertsDF['Txn_Count'] = np.arange(1,len(probs)+1)
    ProbAlertsDF['Fraud_Txns_Count'] = np.where(ProbAlertsDF.Fraud==1,1,0)
    ProbAlertsDF['Clean_Txns_Count'] = np.where(ProbAlertsDF.Fraud==0,1/ratio_clean_sampling,0)
    ProbAlertsDF['Cum_Frauds'] = ProbAlertsDF.Fraud_Txns_Count.cumsum()
    ProbAlertsDF['Cum_Cleans'] = ProbAlertsDF.Clean_Txns_Count.cumsum()
    ProbAlertsDF['Cum_txns'] = ProbAlertsDF.Cum_Frauds + ProbAlertsDF.Cum_Cleans
    ProbAlertsDF['DailyAlerts'] = round(ProbAlertsDF['Cum_txns']/totalDays,2)
    df.sort_values(by='Score',inplace=True)
    Alerts = round(df.Alerts,2)
    df['scoreThreshold'] = Alerts.apply(lambda x: ProbAlertsDF.Prob.loc[abs(x-ProbAlertsDF.DailyAlerts).idxmin()])
    normTable = df.loc[:,['Score','scoreThreshold']]
    if (normTable.Score == 0).any():
        normTable.loc[normTable.Score == 0,'scoreThreshold'] = -9999999
    else:
        normTable = pd.concat(normTable,pd.DataFrame({'Score':0,'scoreThreshold':-9999999}))
    return normTable
In [77]:
#create normtable :
df = pd.DataFrame({'Score':np.arange(100,-1,-5),'Alerts':np.array([5,10,20,30,50,70,120,150,250,350,450,550,650,750,850,950,1050,1250,1450,1650,1850])})
normTable = norm_table(df,xgb_pred_test_pmml,64)
In [79]:
#function to covert prob from normtable :

def prob_to_norm_score(prob,normtable):
    """
    Convert probabilities to norm scores based on the thresholds provided in the norm table.
    
    Parameters:
    prob : probability
    normtable : pandas Dataframe - norm table having 2 columns:
                1. Score 2. scoreThreshold
    
    Returns
    -------
    Norm Score
    
    Usage Example
    -------------
    normTable = pd.read_excel("NormTable.xlsx")
    normScores = pd.Series(probs).apply(prob_to_norm_score,args=(normTable,)).values
    """
    normtable.reset_index(drop=True,inplace=True)
    if (prob < normtable.scoreThreshold).any():
        temp = prob < normtable.scoreThreshold
        return normtable.iloc[min(temp[temp].index.values - 1),0]
    else:
        return 100
In [80]:
#covert prob to normalized scores : 
normScores_test = pd.Series(xgb_pred_test).apply(prob_to_norm_score,args=(normTable,)).values

Step 7 - Supplimental Stats For Model Reports ¶

KS Statistics ¶
In [88]:
from sklearn.metrics import roc_curve
import numpy as np

def ks(y_true, y_score):
    fpr, tpr, _ = roc_curve(y_true, y_score)

    diff = np.subtract(tpr, fpr)

    mins, maxs = diff.min(), diff.max()

    return mins if abs(mins) > abs(maxs) else maxs
In [89]:
# Train
ks(y_train,xgb_pred_train)
Out[89]:
0.6555087522772088
In [90]:
# Test
ks(y_test,xgb_pred_test)
Out[90]:
0.43932315581723147

Sensitivity Analysis ¶
In [94]:
basic_average = xgb_pred_test.mean()

test_data = X_test.copy()
col_change = test_data.columns[(test_data.nunique() >0)]
col_change = col_change.tolist()

val_change = [-0.2, -0.1, 0, 0.1, 0.2]

sens_final = pd.DataFrame(np.zeros(shape = (1,4)))
sens_final.replace(0,np.nan,inplace = True)
sens_final.columns = ['Variable','Input_change','Average_score','Output_change']

for i in range(len(col_change)):
    for j in range(len(val_change)):
        print(col_change[i],val_change[j])
        changed_test = test_data.copy()
        changed_test.loc[:,col_change[i]] = changed_test[col_change[i]] * (1 + val_change[j])
        pred_s = xgb.predict_proba(changed_test)[:,1]
        df = {'Variable' : [col_change[i]], 'Input_change' : val_change[j] ,'Average_score' : pred_s.mean(), 'Output_change' : (pred_s.mean()/basic_average) - 1  }
        df = pd.DataFrame(df)
        sens_final = pd.concat([sens_final,df])
